window_summary: This window tracks the recent microsoft/vscode release stream from October 2025 through May 2026, including monthly feature releases, recovery updates, and weekly stable releases. Key themes include the introduction of multi-agent development capabilities, agent management tools, and productivity enhancements.

chunks.title: November 2025
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: microsoft/vscode
Release: November 2025
Tag: 1.107.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-12-10T18:28:37Z; created_at: 2025-12-10T18:02:22Z
Release notes:
Welcome to the November 2025 release of Visual Studio Code. There are many updates in this version that we hope you'll like, some of the key highlights include:
VS Code now includes Agent HQ, background agents and custom agents for your org.
* **Agent HQ**: You can use GitHub Copilot alongside your other coding agents to complete multiple tasks. Leverage background, cloud, or local agents and their relative strengths for the right job, all at the same time.
* **Background agents** work in their own isolated workspaces, managed for you by VS Code.
* **Custom agents for organizations:** Your team can now share custom agents at the org level.
![VS Code November 2025 release image](https://code.visualstudio.com/assets/updates/1_107/release-highlights-stable.webp)
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the microsoft/vscode release 1.107.0 (November 2025), VS Code introduces Agent HQ, background agents, and custom agents for organizations. Agent HQ allows using GitHub Copilot alongside other coding agents to complete multiple tasks, leveraging background, cloud, or local agents. Background agents work in isolated workspaces managed by VS Code. Custom agents can be shared at the org level.

chunks.title: December 2025
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: microsoft/vscode
Release: December 2025
Tag: 1.108.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-01-08T20:52:00Z; created_at: 2026-01-08T12:59:30Z
Release notes:
Welcome to the December 2025 release of Visual Studio Code.
![December 2025 Release Image](https://code.visualstudio.com/assets/updates/1_108/release-highlights.webp)
Traditionally, the month of December is a time where our team focuses on cleaning up GitHub issues and pull requests across our repositories. This year, we managed to reduce our open issues by nearly 6,000 and triaged over a thousand more.
In addition to our housekeeping efforts, we have also made several improvements and feature updates across various areas of VS Code.
Happy Coding!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the microsoft/vscode release 1.108.0 (December 2025), the team focused on cleaning up GitHub issues and pull requests, reducing open issues by nearly 6,000 and triaging over a thousand more. Several improvements and feature updates were made across various areas of VS Code.

chunks.title: January 2026
chunks.raw_text: Repository: microsoft/vscode Release: January 2026 Tag: 1.109.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-02-04T21:08:10Z; created_at: 2026-02-04T01:55:52Z Release notes: Welcome to the January 2026 release of Visual Studio Code. In this release, we are further evolving VS Code to make it the **home for multi-agent development**. * **Chat UX**: chat just feels better and snappier with faster streaming, improved reasoning results, and a revamped editor inline chat * **Agent Session Management**: it's now easier to delegate tasks to agents across local, background, and cloud and jump in when needed * **Agent Customization**: build your own workflows using agent orchestrations, and have consistent results with Agent Skills and organization-wide customizations * **Agent Extensibility**: reuse your knowledge with Claude agent support and new Anthropic model capabilities, and enjoy rich chat interactions with MCP Apps * **Agent Optimizations**: agents work smarter with Copilot Memory and experience faster code search with external indexing * **Agent Security & Trust**: feel confident running terminal commands with sandboxing and effective auto-approval rules * **Workbench & productivity**: test your apps without leaving the editor with the new integrated browser
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the microsoft/vscode release 1.109.0 (January 2026), VS Code evolves to become the home for multi-agent development. Highlights include improved Chat UX with faster streaming and revamped editor inline chat, Agent Session Management for delegating tasks to local, background, and cloud agents, Agent Customization with orchestrations and Agent Skills, Agent Extensibility with Claude agent support and MCP Apps, Agent Optimizations with Copilot Memory and external indexing, Agent Security & Trust with sandboxing and auto-approval rules, and a new integrated browser for testing apps without leaving the editor.

chunks.title: February 2026
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: microsoft/vscode
Release: February 2026
Tag: 1.110; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-04T19:17:58Z; created_at: 2026-03-04T00:50:04Z
Release notes:
Welcome to the February 2026 release of Visual Studio Code. This release makes agents practical for longer-running and more complex tasks, giving you more control and visibility, new ways to extend agents, and smarter session management.
[Agent plugins](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_agent-plugins-experimental): install prepackaged bundles of skills, tools, and hooks from the Extensions view
[Agentic browser tools](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_agentic-browser-tools-experimental): let the agent drive the browser to interact with your app and verify its own changes
[Session memory](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_session-memory-for-plans): persist plans and guidance across conversation turns
[Context compaction](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_context-compaction): manually compact conversation history to free up context space
[Fork a chat session](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_fork-a-chat-session): create a new, independent session that inherits conversation history to explore alternative approaches
[Agent Debug panel](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_agent-debug-panel-preview): get real-time visibility into agent events, tool calls, and loaded customizations
[Chat accessibility](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_accessibility): use chat to its fullest with screen reader improvements, keyboard navigation, and notification signals
[Create agent customizations from chat](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_create-agent-customizations-from-chat): generate prompts, skills, agents, and hooks directly from a conversation
[Kitty graphics protocol](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110#_terminal): render high-fidelity images directly in the integrated terminal
For the complete release notes go to [Updates](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_110) on [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/).
Insiders: Want to see new features as soon as possible? You can download the nightly [Insiders](https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders) build and try the latest updates as soon as they are available. And for the latest Visual Studio Code news, updates, and content, follow us on Twitter [@code](https://twitter.com/code)!
Happy Coding!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the microsoft/vscode release 1.110 (February 2026), the release makes agents practical for longer-running and more complex tasks. New features include Agent plugins (prepackaged bundles of skills, tools, and hooks), Agentic browser tools (agent drives browser to interact with app and verify changes), Session memory (persist plans across conversation turns), Context compaction (manually compact conversation history), Fork a chat session (create independent session inheriting history), Agent Debug panel (real-time visibility into agent events), Chat accessibility improvements, Create agent customizations from chat, and Kitty graphics protocol for high-fidelity images in the terminal.

chunks.title: March 2026 (version 1.111)
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: microsoft/vscode
Release: March 2026 (version 1.111)
Tag: 1.111.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2026-03-09T11:05:00Z; created_at: 2026-03-06T23:06:10Z
Release notes:
Welcome to the 1.111 release of Visual Studio Code, **the first of our weekly Stable releases**! This release further enhances the agent experience with agent permissions, agent-scoped hooks, easier agent debugging and troubleshooting, and more.
Happy Coding!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the microsoft/vscode release 1.111.0 (March 2026, version 1.111), this is the first of the weekly Stable releases. It further enhances the agent experience with agent permissions, agent-scoped hooks, easier agent debugging and troubleshooting, and more.

questions.query_text: In the microsoft/vscode releases, which release introduces Agent HQ, background agents, and custom agents for organizations?

answer_support.answer_text: VS Code introduces Agent HQ, background agents, and custom agents for organizations.

questions.query_text: Across the microsoft/vscode releases, how does the agent-related change described in 1.110 (February 2026) differ from the change described in 1.109.0 (January 2026)?

options.text: 1.110 introduces Agent plugins and Agentic browser tools, while 1.109.0 introduces Agent Session Management and Agent Customization.
1.110 introduces Agent HQ, while 1.109.0 introduces background agents.
Both releases focus on issue cleanup and recovery fixes.
1.110 introduces the first weekly Stable release, while 1.109.0 introduces the integrated browser.

questions.expected_answer: 1.110 introduces Agent plugins and Agentic browser tools, while 1.109.0 introduces Agent Session Management and Agent Customization.

answer_support.answer_text: Agent plugins (prepackaged bundles of skills, tools, and hooks), Agentic browser tools (agent drives browser to interact with app and verify changes)
Agent Session Management for delegating tasks to local, background, and cloud agents, Agent Customization with orchestrations and Agent Skills

questions.query_text: In the microsoft/vscode release window, order the following release-content states from earliest to latest: (1) a release that introduces Agent HQ and background agents, (2) a release that introduces Agent plugins and Agentic browser tools, (3) a release that is the first weekly Stable release with agent permissions and agent-scoped hooks, (4) a release that fixes GitHub Copilot CLI not working from VS Code.

answer_support.answer_text: VS Code introduces Agent HQ, background agents, and custom agents for organizations.
Agent plugins (prepackaged bundles of skills, tools, and hooks), Agentic browser tools
first of the weekly Stable releases ... agent permissions, agent-scoped hooks
fixes GitHub Copilot CLI not working from VS Code
